[[File:CHoyleFiringHisSniperRifleAtPhillip.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle shoots [[Philip]] in the leg with his rifle]] Despite Hoyle's best efforts to keep them at bay, several of the crew had managed to get into the building he was in, forcing Hoyle to take cover while they then got onto the roof and began searching for him. Hoyle then called out to the mercenaries, telling them to back off or he would be forced to kill them, which he did not want to do. As the mercenaries had continued to shoot at him, Hoyle got out from cover and fired a shot that hit [[Philip]] in the leg, and caused all the others to take cover, while Hoyle had promised that he would shoot to kill next time, as two men retreated, leaving Phillip bleeding from his leg. [[File:CHoyleHoldingPhillip'sDeadBody.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle witnesses [[Philip]] dying in his arms]] Hoyle then found Phillip, who tried to reach for his gun, which Hoyle moved away from him before noticing that he had accidentally shot Phillip in an artery, causing a lot of bleeding. Knowing that Phillip was losing too much blood, Hoyle immediately removed his belt to try to stop the blood flow, as Phillip was clearly terrified and begged Hoyle to save his life. However, Phillip had already lost too much blood and died in Hoyle's arms, as Hoyle wept. While Hoyle escaped the scene, Castle was arrested by the [[New York City Police Department]], having failed to kill Russo and seemingly killed the girls in the crossfire.{{Ref|TP210}} ====Aftermath of the Ambush==== [[File:CHoyleArrivesInTheVan-S2E11.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle arrives in the van after the [[Ambush at the Valhalla|ambush]]]] {{Quote|I know I killed a [[Philip|man]] and shot others. That's what I know. I've held men as they were dying, many men... but never one that I put there myself, not one with my bullet still in him. I never realized how different it would feel.|Curtis Hoyle to [[Amy Bendix]]|The Abyss}} In the wake of the [[Ambush at the Valhalla|ambush]] at [[Valhalla (Hideout)|Valhalla]], Hoyle drove to the [[Campervan Hideout|campervan]], warning [[Amy Bendix]] that he was coming to ensure that she did not try and shoot him again. Once he stepped inside, Bendix lowered her shotgun before asking where [[Frank Castle]] was, as Hoyle remained silent as he sat down while Bendix then continued asking where Castle was, questioning if Hoyle was hurt or if Castle was dead, which Hoyle denied. [[File:BendixHoyle-TalkingInsideTheVan-S2E11.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle tells [[Amy Bendix]] what had happened]] Hoyle then confessed to not knowing exactly what happened, but he knew that something had gone wrong during the ambush, as Bendix questioned why he would just leave him behind. However, Hoyle then told Bendix that the [[New York City Police Department]] arrested Castle and taken him to [[Sacred Saints Hospital]], much to Bendix's horror as she questioned what state Castle was in when the police had taken him into custody. Hoyle had then admitted that he did not know how badly hurt Castle was, or if it was [[Billy Russo]] who had hurt him, while Hoyle continued to struggle to deal with everything that happened. [[File:CHoyleTalkingAboutKillingPhillip.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle lamenting on [[Philip]]'s painful death]] As Bendix continued to push him for answers, Hoyle told her that he had killed [[Philip]] during the ambush and shot other men. Hoyle went on to tell Bendix that while he had held other men in his arms while they died during his time in the [[United States Armed Forces|Armed Forces]], this was the first time he had been responsible for a man's death, as he confessed that he had not expected it to feel as different for him. While Hoyle remained seated, he then witnessed Bendix grab the radio as she tried to learn more about what happened to Castle, as she heard the news report explaining how Castle had been arrested after killing several men. [[File:CHoyleBeingDoneWithThePunisher.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle tells [[Amy Bendix]] that he will not help]] Having confirmed where Castle was, Bendix insisted that they had to rescue him, as Hoyle told her to not consider something as foolish, although she warned that every hitman in [[New York City]] would target him in order to get [[John Pilgrim]]'s five-million-dollar bounty. Becoming frustrated, Hoyle stood up and insisted that helping Castle was what had put them in this situation in the first place, questioning what their plan would be even if they went to save him as they could not go into the hospital with their guns blazing to rescue him. Hoyle told Bendix that Castle was done, noting that he was also done helping him.{{Ref|TP211}} ====[[Ambush on Amy Bendix]]==== [[File:CHoyleRealizesThatABendixHasGone.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle realizes that [[Amy Bendix]] has left him]] {{Quote|I have no quarrel with you, but I also have very little time. So, if they're your friends, and you're thinking of showing them loyalty, of protecting them, it would be better if you didn't."
"Still, I don't know anything about any [[Punisher|Castle]] or [[Amy Bendix|girl]].|[[John Pilgrim]] and Curtis Hoyle|Collision Course (The Punisher)}} Needing to clear his head in the aftermath of everything that he had witnessed, Hoyle went take his shower. However, once Hoyle then stepped out of the shower, he had discovered that [[Amy Bendix]] had taken the opportunity to leave the [[Campervan Hideout|campervan]], in order to go to [[Sacred Saints Hospital]] and try and break [[Frank Castle]] out of the police's custody, much to Hoyle's considerable frustration, although he still decided to remain in the camper van and not join Bendix in getting Castle freed, fearing the impact Castle was having on him.{{Ref|TP211}} [[File:CHoyleBeingPutOnGunpoint-S2E12.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle witnesses the arrival of [[John Pilgrim]]]] While he remained in the camper van, Hoyle heard the door open as he was faced with [[John Pilgrim]], who held a gun up to Hoyle and had blocked him from getting ahold of the shotgun, as Pilgrim questioned if Hoyle had been expecting trouble, to which Hoyle noted that there was no telling who would burst through his front door, which Pilgrim had then smiled at. Hoyle commented that Pilgrim did not look too good, due to many facial bruises, as Pilgrim noted that it had been a testing day, before Pilgrim invited Hoyle to sit down, as Hoyle did as instructed while Pilgrim had continued to keep his gun aimed at him. [[File:JPilgrimTellsCHoyle-NeedsCastle&Girl.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle is forced to sit down by [[John Pilgrim]]]] As Pilgrim began to search around the camper van, Hoyle considered making a run for his shotgun, only for Pilgrim to threaten to shoot him in the kneecap if he tried. Pilgrim then took his seat opposite Hoyle, clearly in pain from all of his injuries, as Pilgrim then asked how Hoyle knew [[Dinah Madani]]. Remaining confident, Hoyle smiled and claimed that Madani only knew him as her sexual partner, attempting to have himself dismissed as an unimportant character in the story. However, Pilgrim told Hoyle that he believed that he was lying to him, as Hoyle tried to offer Pilgrim anything that he wanted in the camper van. [[File:CHoyleSeesABendixReturn-S2E12.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle trying to lie directly to [[John Pilgrim]]]] Pilgrim asked Hoyle about Castle and Bendix, as Hoyle continued to deny any knowledge of them. Becoming frustrated by Hoyle's lack of answers, Pilgrim warned him that while he did not have a quarrel with him, he also had little time, as he insisted that it would be better for both of them if Hoyle stopped trying to protect his friends. As Hoyle still denied knowing Castle or Bendix, Pilgrim compared him to {{WPS|Saint Peter}}, noting how he would have judged Hoyle without knowing him better a few years ago, insisting that he could now see Hoyle clearly and admired his bravery, but he had still believed he was lying. [[File:CHoyle-vs-JPilgrim-FightingForTheGun.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle saving [[Amy Bendix]] from [[John Pilgrim]]]] However, just as Pilgrim took out a pair of knuckledusters to torture Hoyle with, they were interrupted as Bendix had burst into the room, excitedly exclaiming that Castle had been freed from the hospital. As the group stared in stunned silence for a moment, Bendix grabbed the shotgun as Hoyle charged at Pilgrim and pushed him against the wall, preventing him from aiming his gun at Bendix. As Pilgrim then pushed Bendix to the ground, Hoyle was able to knock the gun out from his hands before being knocked down, as Bendix then fired the shotgun, missing Hoyle and painfully shooting Pilgrim in the leg. [[File:CHoylePicksUpJPilgrimViolently.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle violently battles against [[John Pilgrim]]]] Despite his injury, Pilgrim dived after Bendix, attempting to drag her away, only for Hoyle to grab Pilgrim by the leg as he then pulled him away from her, as he managed to throw Pilgrim across the room and began beating him down. As Hoyle had continued punching Pilgrim, Bendix had then managed to escape the camper van and ran away. Seeing this, Pilgrim knocked Hoyle back and attempted to run after her, as Hoyle pushed him against the wall and slammed him through the table, only for Pilgrim to punch him directly in the throat, causing Hoyle to fall backwards away from him while he gasped for air. [[File:CHoyleIsDefeatedByJPilgrim.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle is swiftly defeated by [[John Pilgrim]]]] Pilgrim then used this momentary distraction to his advantage, as he pushed Hoyle into the wall and began punching him repeatedly while wearing the knuckledusters, with Hoyle struggling to defend himself from the brutal attacks. Despite his best efforts, Hoyle was beaten to submission due to all these repeated hits with the knuckledusters, as Pilgrim ended the fight by smashing him through the wardrobe before finally striking him across the face with the shotgun, which had then knocked Hoyle unconscious. However, rather than simply execute a defenseless Hoyle, Pilgrim just walked out of the camper van.{{Ref|TP212}} ====[[Kidnapping of David Schultz]]==== [[File:CHoyleBeingPickedUpFromKnockout.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle is found and wakened by [[Frank Castle]]]] {{Quote|Who the hell is that?"
"It's [[David Schultz]]."
"[[United States Congress|U.S. Senator]] Schultz?"
"Yeah, he keeps telling me that."
"Jesus, Frank!|Curtis Hoyle and [[Frank Castle]]|Collision Course (The Punisher)}} Hoyle was eventually found by [[Frank Castle]], who woke him up and sat him up, questioning what had happened with [[Amy Bendix]], while Hoyle tried to get his senses back and managed to explain that [[John Pilgrim]] had attacked them. As Castle then demanded to know if Pilgrim had taken Bendix, Hoyle could only explain that he had fought to protect her, and allowed her to run away, while Castle expressed his fury by kicking over their table before grabbing his gun. [[File:CHoyleFCastleSearchingForABendixsPhone.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle and [[Frank Castle]] look for [[Amy Bendix]]]] While he sat himself upright and readjusted his prosthetic leg, Hoyle gave Castle his phone so he could call Bendix, only for them to hear the phone ringing close by. Hoyle had then followed Castle outside as they searched for Bendix, finding the phone was dropped during her escape from Pilgrim. As they tried to remain calm, Hoyle watched as Castle then silently slammed the phone down, before he marched over to his car, only to drag a terrified [[David Schultz]] out and into the [[Campervan Hideout|campervan]], as the horrified Hoyle questioned who he was and why Castle had kidnapped him, with Castle refusing to answer him. [[File:CHoyleSeesFCastleKidnappingTheSen..jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle sees [[David Schultz]] being kidnapped]] Upon realizing who Schultz was, Hoyle expressed his horror at Castle for kidnapping a Senator, as Hoyle then sat down and watched while Castle interrogated Schultz. Hoyle listened on while Castle explained that he had wanted to find Pilgrim in order to save Bendix, although Schultz insisted that he did not know what Castle was talking about, resulting in Castle stepping into the other room before coming back with the bag filled with evidence of Schultz's homosexual relationship which had led to the entire situation, as Castle vowed to kill Schultz if anything happened to Bendix, which had greatly worried Hoyle. [[File:CHoyleSittingInCamperVan.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle watches Castle interrogating Schultz]] Hoyle watched as Schultz questioned if they intended to blackmail him, as he noted how Castle had assaulted him inside his home and begun yelling about murders and killers, which he had insisted that he knew nothing about. Horrified at seeing Schultz in such distress, Hoyle stepped into their kitchen as he glared at Castle. Once Hoyle returned, Castle told Schultz about how [[Nikolai Poloznev]] had paid for the photographs of Schultz and his lover, resulting with everyone involved being murdered under the orders from [[Anderson Schultz|Anderson]] and [[Eliza Schultz]], much to the complete horror of Schultz, who had started to weep as he insisted that he had never been part in any of this.{{Ref|TP212}} [[File:DSchultz-CHoyle-FCastle-Facetime.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle listening to the [[Anderson Schultz|Schu]][[Eliza Schultz|ltz]]'s phone call]] Hoyle then sat back and watched while Castle used Schultz's phone to then call his parents, revealing to Anderson that Schultz had been kidnapped, demanding that Pilgrim return Bendix to him in exchange for Schultz's safe return to them. Hoyle watched on as the Schultzes threatened Castle's life, while he made it clear that he knew about their murders of [[Sergei Konchevsky]] and [[Fiona's Crew]], which they did not even deny, warning they would ensure that Castle died slowly if he did not release their son, resulting in Castle then punching Schulz across the face to show his own power with their current situation. [[File:CHoyleGetsACallFromABendix.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle suddenly gets a call from [[Amy Bendix]]]] With Hoyle remaining silent throughout the exchange, he watched as Castle made a deal with Eliza that once Pilgrim delivered Bendix to him, they would get Schultz back, telling them to call him up on that phone and promising that if they dared to try and trace their call and come after them, then he would kill Schultz, before hanging up their call, while Schultz questioned if this was a threat or a fact. Eventually, Hoyle picked up a call from Bendix, informing them that she had not been taken, and instead knew where Pilgrim was, as Castle left Hoyle to guard Schultz while he went to save Bendix and kill Pilgrim.{{Ref|TP213}} ====Liberation of David Schultz==== [[File:CHoyleTalksToDSchultz-1-S2E13.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle tells [[David Schultz]] that he is a pawn]] {{Quote|Last time I saw you, Mr. Hoyle you had a rifle pointed at my head."
"I'm sorry about that."
"You should be. Where's [[Frank Castle]]?"
"I don't know. But what I do know is this shit needs to end. That's why I'm here. That's why [[David Schultz|he]]'s here.|[[Brett Mahoney]] and Curtis Hoyle|The Whirlwind}} As Castle left in order to save [[Amy Bendix]], Hoyle sat alone inside the [[Campervan Hideout]] with [[David Schultz]], who had continued to insist that he did not want any of this violence to happen because of him, with Hoyle claiming that he could relate to that feeling. Schultz noted how his [[Eliza Schultz|mother]] and [[Anderson Schultz|father]] had not even denied those accusations against them and questioned how he could not have realize what they were truly capable of. Hoyle had then told Schultz that he had been his parent's poster boy, and they wanted to keep him clean of all their crimes so he could deny any knowledge. [[File:CHoyleTalksToDSchultz-2-S2E13.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle tells Schultz that his [[Anderson Schultz|par]][[Eliza Schultz|ents]] are bad]] Hoyle then listened on as Schultz insisted that he did not ask for any deniability, noting how people had been [[Schultz Conspiracy|murdered]] because of some photographs of him and his lover, noting that his true homosexuality should not be relevant, which Hoyle agreed with. As Schultz realized that his parent's homophobia was the reason for all of the violence, Hoyle noted that he clearly had bad parents, as Schultz had told him about the lessons his mother had taught him about prejudice, as he questioned if they had gotten these people murdered because they were ashamed of him, fearing it would hurt their legacy. [[File:CHoyle-IJustWannaGoHome-S2E13.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle saying that he just wants to go home]] Schultz then turned to Hoyle and noted that he did not seem to be a lot like [[Frank Castle]], as he then questioned why Hoyle was holding him hostage. When Schultz questioned if this was who Hoyle was or who he wanted to become, Hoyle had then simply insisted that he had started out by just wanting to do the right thing, although now he had only wanted to go [[Curtis Hoyle's Apartment|home]], which Schultz laughed at as he agreed with this desire. Seeing the effect that his involvement with Castle's crusade was having and how it was bringing him down a darker path than he wanted to be on, Hoyle decided that he would free Schultz. [[File:BMahoneyCHoyle-MeetEachOther.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle being reunited with [[Brett Mahoney]]]] Hoyle had then contacted [[Brett Mahoney]], asking him to meet him at a racetrack, telling him to go there alone. Once Mahoney had arrived, Hoyle greeted him, while Mahoney had then reminded Hoyle that the last time they had seen one another, Hoyle had a rifle pointed at his head, having just rescued Castle from getting arrested following the shootout with [[Billy Russo]]. While Hoyle apologized for this, Mahoney had insisted that he should be sorry before questioning where Castle was, although Hoyle then insisted that he did not know, although he instead claimed that he knew that all of the violence had to end. [[File:TP213-MahoneyHoyleSchultz.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle giving [[David Schultz]] over to Mahoney]] Hoyle then revealed that Schultz was with him, much to Mahoney's shock, while Mahoney questioned if he was alright to which Schultz insisted that he had been treated well considering the situation that he had been put in. Hoyle then confirmed that Castle had kidnapped Schultz, although he also told Mahoney that Schultz had nothing to do with Russo and that investigation, but he just wanted to give him back to ensure that Schultz remained safe. As Mahoney had asked Schultz if he wanted to have Hoyle arrested, Schultz insisted that he did not, claiming that Hoyle was just trying to do the right thing. [[File:CHoyleAgreesToStayAwayFromTrouble.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle agrees to stay away from any trouble]] As Mahoney complained that over the last few days, he had learned that nothing was easy nor simple when Castle was involved, before commenting that he had looked up Hoyle's record which seemed to confirm that he was a good man. Mahoney then promised that Hoyle would be allowed to walk free, under their agreement that he never threaten another [[New York City Police Department]] officer ever again. As Mahoney agreed to take Schultz back to his [[David Schultz's Apartment|apartment]], he also advised Hoyle to also return home and leave Castle behind him, with Hoyle insisting that there was nothing that he would rather do.{{Ref|TP213}} ====Called by Billy Russo==== [[File:TP213-C.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle answers a phone call from [[Billy Russo]]]] {{Quote|Can you promise me that you won't call the [[New York City Police Department|cops]], at least? Can you do that? Can you do that?"
"I promise, I won't call the cops."
"Look, I know that I probably don't deserve it after what I've done but if you can find it in yourself to be a brother one last time. I don't want to die alone.|[[Billy Russo]] and Curtis Hoyle|The Whirlwind}} Once the whole situation with [[David Schultz]] was finished, Hoyle had then finally returned to his [[Curtis Hoyle's Apartment|apartment]] and attempted to get some much-needed sleep. Much to Hoyle's surprise, he was then unexpectedly called up by [[Billy Russo]], who informed Hoyle that he was dying. Hoyle asked Russo where he was, and Russo had explained that he reached the [[St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church]] and at this moment, he was dying from blood loss due to [[Dinah Madani]] having shot him multiple times during their fight. [[File:TP213-B.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle speaks to a slowly dying [[Billy Russo]]]] Russo had asked Hoyle to come into the church to help him, only for Hoyle remained silent as he considered what he should do due to his conflict with Russo. Understanding that Hoyle was not going to help him, Russo instead asked him not to inform the [[New York City Police Department]] about him what Hoyle then accepted. Russo then asked Hoyle to share his last moments, understanding he did not deserve it, Russo did not want to die alone. As Russo hanged up, Hoyle kept his promises, instead, he informed [[Frank Castle]] about where Russo was, so Castle had then arrived at the church and finally killed Russo.{{Ref|TP213}} ====End of Russo's Case==== [[File:MadaniMahoneyHoyle-FindingRussoDead.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle looking down at [[Billy Russo]]'s corpse]] {{Quote|What part of this isn't clear to you?"
"Only who fired the bullets. Tell me how it went again?"
"[[Billy Russo|Billy]] called me. Then I called you."
"That's right. Because you're done being involved with [[Frank Castle|Frank]].|Curtis Hoyle and [[Brett Mahoney]]|The Whirlwind}} Following [[Billy Russo]]'s demise, Hoyle had then responded by calling up [[Brett Mahoney]] as well as [[Dinah Madani]] and told them that Russo had died, and to meet him inside the [[St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church]]. There, they discovered Russo's body, while Hoyle took a moment to close the eyes of his former friend. Mahoney wanted to preserve the crime scene; however, Hoyle had insisted that the details about Russo's death should be clear. At first, Mahoney expressed doubt on the situation, asking who fired those last two bullets. [[File:TP213-CaseClosed.png|{{AltFloat}}|Hoyle denies knowledge about [[Frank Castle]]]] When asked to explain exactly what had happened again, Hoyle told Mahoney how Russo called him and then Hoyle called Mahoney, as he chose not to mention calling [[Frank Castle]], aa Mahoney noted that Hoyle should be done with Castle. Madani then casually claimed that she could have possibly fired five bullets and killed Russo, stating the case was now closed. As Mahoney asked about Castle and whether or not he was the one who had actually shot and killed Russo, both Hoyle and Madani had mockingly denied knowledge of Castle, much to Mahoney's frustration, as he then walked out of the church.{{Ref|TP213}} ===[[Snap]]=== Later [[2018|that year]], [[Thanos]]' [[Snap]] occurred, which killed off half of humanity,{{Ref|AIW}} although Hoyle was one of the survivors.[https://www.instagram.com/p/DNis-9YRfX4/ Jason Moore on Instagram - August 19, 2025] ==Abilities== {{Quote|Back in the day, you know, I would have snapped [[Lewis Wilson|him]] in two.|Curtis Hoyle to [[Frank Castle]]|Front Toward Enemy}} *'''Expert Combatant''': As a member of the US military, Hoyle was trained at close combat. At his skirmish with [[Lewis Wilson]], Hoyle managed to overpower him, however, Wilson used his prosthetic leg to beat him. He also managed to fight [[John Pilgrim]] before getting defeated. {{Quote|Next time I'm shooting to kill. Get your asses out of here!|Curtis Hoyle to [[Philip]]|The Dark Hearts of Men}} [[File:CurtisTakesAimSniper.PNG|thumb|250px|Hoyle taking aim at [[Jigsaw]] from a building]] *'''Expert Marksman''': Hoyle is skilled in the use of pistols and sniper rifles. He was close to shooting [[Billy Russo]] in the head with a rifle he stole, and fired a warning shot at [[Brett Mahoney]]. He later used the same rifle to cover the [[Punisher]] as he entered Valhalla. His medical expertise gives him knowledge of where to place bullets for a non-lethal effect, doing so with [[Philip]]. {{Quote|You're still the best corpsman I ever saw. Hell, I bet you'd have stitched your own damn leg back on, given a chance.|[[Billy Russo]] to Curtis Hoyle|Kandahar (episode)}} *'''Expert Physician''': Hoyle has been trained in the ability to perform medical procedures and heal those that have been wounded. In particular, he mentioned being tested to patch a goat during his time spent in the Navy. After nearly escaping a trap and suffering injuries, Hoyle was summoned by [[Micro]] to aid [[Frank Castle]], extracting an arrowhead from his body and patching the wound. {{Expand}} *'''Businessman''': ''To be added'' ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== {{Quote|You gonna kill me with my own gun?|Curtis Hoyle to [[Billy Russo]]|Memento Mori}} *'''{{WPS|Kimber Warrior}}''': Hoyle owned this handgun, that he kept under his pillow, until it was taken by [[Billy Russo]], in an ambush to force Hoyle give up the whereabouts of [[Frank Castle]]. Russo kept threatening Hoyle with his own gun, and ended up shooting him in the shoulder with it. *'''{{WPS|Kimber Warrior|Kimber Warrior SOC}}''': Hoyle obtained this handgun after he lost his previous one, and kept it around once [[Billy Russo]] escaped from [[Sacred Saints Hospital]]. He took the handgun when he heard an unexpected knock on his door, but instead of Russo, it turned out to be [[Frank Castle]]. He carried it when he was ambushed by Russo, who forced him at gunpoint to eject the magazine and the bullet in the chamber, despite Russo told him later that he had no intention of harming him, and also when he asked Castle and [[Dinah Madani]] to meet him at the [[St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church]] to discuss what to do with Russo. As he went with Castle to investigate a couple of leads, he carried the handgun to break into [[Jake Nelson]]'s apartment, and Russo's [[Jigsaw's Hideout|hideout]]. *'''{{WPS|Remington R-25}}''': Hoyle took the rifle from [[Philip]], and he managed to locate [[Billy Russo]] and aimed at him, but Russo moved before being he was able to shoot him. He then took the rifle to the alley where [[Brett Mahoney]] was about to arrest [[Frank Castle]], firing a warning shot near Mahoney and threatening him so that Castle could escape. Hoyle used the rifle to watch over [[Valhalla (Hideout)|Valhalla]] in order to prepare for [[Frank Castle]]'s attack on the compound, not knowing that [[Billy Russo]] was watching them. In order to distract the men inside, Hoyle detonated a bomb to allow Castle to enter, and he started shooting at the men that came out of the building. He defended himself from the men that entered reached the roof where he was, using the rifle to accurately shoot at them, but not shooting to kill, offering them the chance to leave. However, [[Philip]] had been critically wounded by the shot, and died despite Hoyle's efforts to save him. ===Other Equipment=== {{Quote|Don't be a wallowing asshole.
